results.title: Is Sprott Inc. (SII) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
excerpts:
Section Title: Is Sprott Inc. (SII) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
Content:
This structural advantage has driven strong investor preference, capturing over 100% of net inflows in U.S.-listed physical silver trusts since 2021, demonstrating the credibility and stickiness of its model. Sprott’s early and strategic entry into uranium through the Physical Uranium Trust and the Sprott Uranium Miners ETF has become a growth engine, with assets surging from under $400 million at acquisition to over $4.4 billion by Q3 2025, reflecting the global reemergence of nuclear energy and the company’s foresight in building institutional infrastructure ahead of the trend.
Financially, Sprott operates a highly capital-efficient model, with AUM reaching $49.1 billion by September 2025 and crossing $50 billion in October, delivering a 37% increase in full-year earnings, 62% adjusted EBITDA margins, and a 33% dividend increase, underscoring the durability and scalability of its platform.

Section Title: Sprott Inc. (SII-T) > Sprott Inc. (SII-T) Jul 02, 2025
Content:
Share Watch
A play on natural resources. Believes we're in a precious metals bull market. This name is a financial services brand name for gold and silver (as well as for uranium). Also active in many other materials, including the Sprott Physical Uranium Trust. Expanding from precious metals to the whole natural resources complex.
Assets under management now ~$40B USD and growing fairly rapidly. As assets grow, FCF from management fees will be enormous. Yield is 1.79%.
(Disclaimer:  He's a former employee and a former director of Sprott. Also the current largest shareholder at almost 10% of the company.)
*(Analysts’ price target is $87.13)*

Most investors need some help investing in natural resources. Sprott is a brand name for natural resources, in physical trusts and in equities management. Disclosure: he's a former employee and former director. He's a large shareholder, and may even be the largest one. But it's a stock he thinks every natural resource investor ought to own.

results.title: Sprott Inc (NYSE:SII) Screened as a Top CAN SLIM Growth Stock | ChartMill.com
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > **Meeting the CAN SLIM Growth Rules**
Content:
The "CAN" part of the name centers on current and yearly earnings growth, which is the basic driver of the method. Sprott Inc shows very good results in these parts, matching O'Neil's rules for faster profit growth.
Section Title: ... > **Meeting the CAN SLIM Growth Rules**
Content:
**Current Quarterly Earnings (C):** The system looks for large or speeding up quarterly earnings. SII reported a very high year-over-year EPS growth of more than 141% in its latest quarter, well above the common lowest point of 20-25%. This fast growth points to strong current momentum.
**Annual Earnings Increases (A):** A record of steady yearly growth is needed to be sure a company is not a short-term event. SII shows a solid 3-year EPS compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of about 55%, much higher than the 25% mark. This verifies a continued history of profit growth.
**Return on Equity (A):** As a gauge of management skill and profit, a high Return on Equity is preferred. SII's ROE of 18.3% is better than the 10% minimum, showing the company is creating good returns on shareholder money.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > **Technical Performance and Market Standing**
Content:
Maybe the most unique part of CAN SLIM is its use of technical study to confirm fundamental strength, shown in the "N" and "L" rules.
**Leader or Laggard (L):** This is judged by relative strength (RS), which compares a stock's price action to the wider market. A high RS score shows market leadership. SII has a very high relative strength score of 98.3, meaning it has done better than 98% of all stocks over the last year. This is a clear sign of a CAN SLIM leader.
**New Highs (N):** The strategy looks for stocks moving to new price highs on high volume, a signal of growing market notice. While present price changes may create specific entry questions, SII's long-term direction is clearly up, and it trades in the higher part of its 52-week range, having given a very high 238% return over the past twelve months.
